errors.excludedReason: You can raise the nRMSE threshold in advanced options, or try other parameters (mass, Crr, position).

dashboard.meanCda: Mean CdA ({{count}} ride kept out of {{total}}) - inverse-variance method
dashboard.meanCda_plural: Mean CdA ({{count}} rides kept out of {{total}}) - inverse-variance method
dashboard.exclusionFooter: Exclusion: nRMSE > 45%, CdA outside [{{min}}-{{max}}] m², or degenerate solver (bound hit / non-identifiable). Hover an excluded ride for the exact reason.

position.more_aero_hint: A more aero position like {{label}} (ref. {{value}}) could save you up to {{gain}} m² - if your body follows.

refTable.cdaLead: Where does your CdA sit compared to reference positions?
refTable.crrLead: Where does your Crr sit compared to reference tyres/surfaces?

fileUpload.dropHint: Multiple rides = more accurate averaged result (bad ones are filtered out automatically)
fileUpload.crrFixHint: Fixing Crr gives a more stable CdA. Select your tyres if you know them.
fileUpload.manualWindHint: Fill these two fields if the verdict shows 'fragile wind' or if you have a reliable measurement (station, Windy, Tempest). Leave empty to keep Open-Meteo.

intervals.title: Intervals.icu
intervals.keepUnfiltered: Keep all rides, don't filter on solver agreement
intervals.errAnalysis: Analysis error - corrupted file or pre-processing failed
intervals.errSolver: Solver disagreement, bound hit, or poorly-conditioned Hessian
intervals.errBounds: CdA estimated outside the physical bounds for this bike type
intervals.flatSpeedTooltip: Average power / CdA = how fast you can go on the flat.

history.title: Analysis history
history.emptyDesc: Run an analysis to see it appear here.
history.disclaimer: Your analyses are stored only in your browser (localStorage), never on a server. They can be lost if you clear the cache or change device - export regularly to keep a backup.
history.exportTitle: Download all analyses in JSON format
history.importTitle: Import a JSON history (merges with existing)

compare.title: Multi-rider comparison
compare.subtitle: Drag-drop multiple files per rider for more accurate averaged results. Formats: .FIT, .GPX, .TCX.
compare.draftingBody: {{drafter}} has a CdA {{pct}}% lower than {{puller}} at similar speed. {{drafter}} probably followed the wheels.
compare.bestAeroTooltip: Lowest CdA (weighted average over all rides).
compare.bestWCdaTooltip: W/CdA = average power / CdA. The fastest rouleur on the flat: combines fitness (watts) and aero (low CdA). The flat-terrain analogue of W/kg.
compare.mostEfficientTooltip: Lowest total drag force (aero + rolling) at 40 km/h.

diag.windModerate: Wind may be imprecise (CdA moderately sensitive)
diag.windBodyFragile: On this ride, varying the API wind by ±30% moves the estimated CdA by more than 0.05 m². Typical of an area where Open-Meteo doesn't capture the real wind (coast, relief, coarse grid). Enter measured wind to make the analysis reliable.
diag.windBodyModerate: Varying the API wind by ±30% moves the CdA by 0.02 to 0.05 m². Moderate sensitivity - a measured wind would improve precision.
diag.windBodyRobust: Varying the API wind by ±30% leaves the CdA almost unchanged. Estimate is robust to wind.
diag.windApiSummary: API wind used: {{ms}} m/s ({{kmh}} km/h) from {{dir}}°
diag.manualWindHelp: Enter the real wind at rider level (not at 10 m). Examples: Météo-France stations, Windy, Weatherflow Tempest, or an estimate based on feel on the bike.
diag.unreliableTitle: CdA and Crr not estimable on this ride (R² = {{r2}})
diag.unreliableBody1: The physical model does not apply: one or more forces are not accounted for (real wind very different from API weather, heavy drafting, dragging brakes, faulty sensor…). The values below are not reliable - the solver found the 'least bad' fit possible, not a correct measurement.
diag.unreliableBody2: What you can still use: the flat / climb / descent breakdown below (still qualitatively informative), and the direction of the residuals bias in the alerts (indicates whether the sensor is over- or under-calibrated).
diag.badFitBody: The physical model does not explain this ride well. Likely causes: miscalibrated power meter, very noisy GPS altitude, lots of drafting or braking, or a ride poorly suited to the method (e-bike, cyclocross, MTB in trees). Treat the CdA and Crr values with strong caution.
diag.pmBiasHigh: **Sensor probably miscalibrated.** On flat pedalled sections ({{n}} points), the measured power is {{pct}}% higher than the theoretical value for a typical CdA/Crr. This is the sign of a sensor with a stuck offset or a missing zero-offset. Recalibrate before the next ride.
diag.pmBiasMild: Measured power {{pct}}% above the theoretical value ({{n}} flat points). May indicate a slight calibration drift, or simply a stronger-than-average rider profile.
diag.pmBiasLow: Measured power {{pct}}% below the theoretical value ({{n}} flat points). Sensor under-reading or strong unmodelled tailwind.

breakdown.title: CdA by gradient regime
breakdown.subtitle: Big asymmetry = sign of bias OR real position change
breakdown.asymmetryWarn: {{spread}} m² spread between regimes - check that the API wind is representative of the ride.

derived.title: Power to ride on the flat
derived.aeroAt40: At 40 km/h: {{share}}% of the power overcomes the air, the rest is rolling resistance.

charts.altitudeDesc: Compares the real GPS altitude (green) to the 'virtual' altitude rebuilt from the energy balance with the estimated CdA/Crr. If the estimates are correct, the two curves stay close. A growing gap indicates a systematic bias (sensor, weight, or wind estimated wrong).
charts.cdaRollingDesc: Instantaneous CdA on a rolling 10-minute window. Should stay roughly flat around the horizontal line (mean CdA). Peaks or dips = position change, drafting, or zones where the model is unreliable.
charts.powerDecompDesc: Breaks down total power into components: aero (apparent wind force), rolling (tyre/ground friction) and gravity (climbs). Lets you see which force dominates at each moment and where an aero improvement would matter most.
charts.powerScatterDesc: Each point compares predicted power (Y axis) to measured power (X axis). Good fit = points aligned on the red diagonal. Scattered = imprecise model on this ride.
charts.residualsDesc: Gap between model and measured power, in watts. Should be centred on 0 and bell-shaped. A systematic offset (mean ≠ 0) suggests bias: miscalibrated sensor, wrong weight, or under/over-estimated wind.
charts.speedPowerDesc: Relationship between ground speed and measured power. Scatter comes from gradient, wind, and acceleration. Compact cloud = uniform conditions. Very scattered = varied ride (mountain, changing wind).
charts.energyPieDesc: Share of total energy spent against air (aero), friction (rolling), gravity (climbs), and acceleration. On the flat above 30 km/h, aero dominates (~70-80%). In the mountains, gravity takes over.
charts.speedCdaDesc: Relationship between instantaneous CdA and measured power. A strong correlation suggests a bias (sensor or Crr estimated wrong) that depends on the power regime.
charts.windChartDesc: Wind speed (blue, km/h) and bearing (red dotted, degrees; 0°=N, 90°=E, 180°=S, 270°=W) along the route. Shows whether wind stayed constant or changed, which directly impacts model quality.
charts.rhoDesc: Air density variation along the route. ρ drops with altitude and rises with cold. Same CdA produces less drag at low ρ (altitude). On a 0-to-1500 m ride, ρ can vary by 15% — that's why it's computed per point.
charts.mapDesc: GPS trace on a basemap. Coloured by rolling CdA (see legend below the map) for a quick read of where the position held or drifted.

chartsLegend.filteredHint: — grey zones = segments excluded by iterative refinement

categoryDesc.Fundamentals: The physics and vocabulary every reader should start with.
categoryDesc.Algorithms: Solvers, filters, and the pipeline architecture.
categoryDesc.Statistics: Priors, meta-analysis, and the regression tests that guard them.
categoryDesc.Diagnostics: What to inspect when a number looks wrong.

power-equation.title: The cycling power equation: Martin et al. (1998)
power-equation.desc: How 4 physical forces (aero, rolling, gravity, acceleration) determine the power you have to produce. The foundation of everything.

cda-what-is-it.title: CdA: what is it and why does it matter?
cda-what-is-it.desc: The aerodynamic drag coefficient explained simply. How it varies with your position, your equipment, and why 0.01 of CdA makes a real difference.

wind-correction.title: Wind correction: from the weather API to wind-inverse
wind-correction.desc: Wind is the biggest source of error. How we correct it: Open-Meteo API, spatial tiling, 10 m → 1 m log profile, and inverse estimation from your data.

solvers.title: The 3 AeroProfile solvers: Martin LS, Chung VE, Wind-Inverse
solvers.desc: Why a single solver isn't enough, and how AeroProfile automatically picks the best method for each ride.

filters.title: Data filtering: how we separate signal from noise
filters.desc: 13 filters, from braking to drafting, and why every excluded point makes the result more reliable.

iterative-refinement.title: Iterative refinement: only use the points where the model works
iterative-refinement.desc: Two automatic passes: solve, flag zones where real / virtual altitude diverge, exclude, re-solve. Like Golden Cheetah, but automated.

drafting-detection.title: Drafting detection: when riding in a group fools the CdA
drafting-detection.desc: Why apparent CdA drops 30-40% in a peloton, how we detect it, and what we can still learn.

yaw-angle.title: Yaw angle: why crosswinds change your CdA
yaw-angle.desc: When the wind hits sideways, your drag rises. How we correct to report a 'wind-tunnel' CdA.

bayesian-priors.title: Bayesian priors: how to stabilise the solver
bayesian-priors.desc: When data is insufficient, a soft prior stops the solver from diverging. Intuitive and mathematical explanation. Includes the multi-ride prior trap.

aggregation-methods.title: Multi-ride aggregation: inverse-variance vs hierarchical
aggregation-methods.desc: How AeroProfile combines N rides into a single representative CdA: precision-weighted mean (method A) and DerSimonian-Laird hierarchical meta-analysis.

prior-invariance.title: Prior invariance: why your position choice should not move the aggregated CdA
prior-invariance.desc: The most powerful consistency test in meta-analysis: rerun the same analysis with two different priors. When invariance breaks it's almost always a convergence bug - and the story of a fix on a noisy 4iiii single-leg dataset.

power-meter-quality.title: The power meter: the error source the solver cannot correct
power-meter-quality.desc: Why single-leg sensors (4iiii, Stages left) give a 2× more variable CdA, and how AeroProfile detects a calibration bias independently of the solver.

w-cda-metric.title: W/CdA: the rouleur's metric (the W/kg for flat terrain)
w-cda-metric.desc: Power over aero drag determines your flat-terrain speed. W/CdA → km/h lookup table included.

intervals-integration.title: Intervals.icu integration: analyse a year of rides in one click
intervals-integration.desc: Connect your account, filter your rides, and get a weighted-average CdA over dozens of rides. The 4 filtering levels explained.

profile.label: Profile:
profile.activeTooltip: Active profile - click to reload its settings
profile.newTooltip: Create a new profile with the current form settings
profile.reloadTooltip: Reload the settings saved in the active profile
profile.saveTooltip: Save the current form settings into the active profile
profile.helpUpload: Saves the mass, bike, position, and Crr. Reloading a profile pre-fills the form fields.
profile.helpIntervals: Saves the Intervals credentials, mass, bike, position, and filters. Reloading a profile pre-fills all these fields.
